note: Header row. Real records start after first REFRESH. Schema: id, url, type (primary|secondary|social), excerpt, fetched_at (ISO8601), source_lane, trust_score (0-1), content_hash

excerpt: OpenAI release page for GPT-5.5. Apr 23, 2026 GA. Terminal-Bench 2.0 82.7%, GDPval 84.9%, SWE-Bench Pro 58.6%, OSWorld-Verified 78.7%, CyberGym 81.8%. 1M context in Codex. $5/M input, $30/M output API. Preparedness Framework High for bio/cyber.

OpenAI Research release index. Confirms GPT-5.5 (Apr 23), GPT-5.5 Instant (May 5), realtime voice models (May 7), OpenAI Privacy Filter (Apr 22), ChatGPT Images 2.0 (Apr 21), GPT-Rosalind (Apr 16), GPT-5.4 (Mar 5), GPT-5.3 Instant (Mar 3), Rosalind Biodefense (May 29), OpenAI on AWS (Jun 1).

LangChain 1.0 + LangGraph 1.0 GA Oct 22, 2025. create_agent abstraction built on LangGraph runtime. Middleware hooks (HITL, summarization, PII redaction). langchain-core promoted to 1.0 with standardized content blocks. Python 3.9 dropped (EOL Oct 2025), requires 3.10+. Legacy code moves to langchain-classic. LangGraph 1.0 has durable state, built-in persistence, first-class HITL. Used by Uber, LinkedIn, Klarna, Rippling, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Cisco. 90M monthly downloads.

Letta Code Dec 16, 2025. #1 model-agnostic OSS harness on TerminalBench, comparable to Claude Code/Gemini CLI/Codex CLI. Memory init via /init, skill learning via /remember. Skills as .md files in git. Vector+FTS+hybrid search. Conversations API Jan 21, 2026. Letta Code app Apr 6, 2026. Context Repositories Feb 12, 2026.

markdown-it demo page. 100% Markdown 1.0, 100% CommonMark 0.31, full GFM. Plugin system: emoji, subscript, superscript, ins, mark, footnotes, definition lists, abbreviations, custom containers (::: classname). Token stream parser. Configurable html / xhtmlOut / breaks / linkify / typographer / highlight. CommonMark strict toggle.

markdown-it GitHub repository. Active 2014-2026, mature JavaScript markdown parser. Token-based AST, plugin architecture, CommonMark + GFM compliant, 10K+ stars. Pluggable renderer. Synchronous parser. Used by Vue.js docs, VuePress, VitePress, Astro, Logseq.

Marked documentation. 'built for speed' - low-level markdown compiler for parsing markdown without caching or blocking. 100% Markdown 1.0, 98% CommonMark 0.31, 97% GFM 0.29. CLI and in-browser. Warning: 'Marked does not sanitize the output HTML.' Tools using marked: zero-md (markdown web component), texme, StrapDown.js, raito, Homebrewery, marked_reader.

Pandoc User's Guide. 'a universal document converter.' 40+ input formats (markdown, commonmark, commonmark_x, gfm, markdown_strict, markdown_phpextra, markdown_mmd, html, latex, docx, rst, etc.). --embed-resources: 'Produce a standalone HTML file with no external dependencies, using data: URIs.' --self-contained is deprecated synonym. -s/--standalone produces valid HTML with <head> and <body>.

GitHub Flavored Markdown Spec, Version 0.29-gfm (2019-04-06). 'GFM is a strict superset of CommonMark.' Extensions: Tables, Task list items, Strikethrough, Autolinks, Disallowed Raw HTML. ATX headings 1-6, Setext headings, fenced code blocks (3+ backticks or tildes).

Quartz 5.0.0 (May 24, 2026 release). 'a fast, batteries-included static-site generator that transforms Markdown content into fully functional websites.' Requires Node v22 + npm v10.9.2. Obsidian compatibility, full-text search, graph view, wikilinks, transclusions, Backlinks, Latex, syntax highlighting, popover previews, Docker, i18n, comments. Hot-reload, incremental rebuilds. SPA routing. 'Ridiculously fast page loads and tiny bundle sizes.'

Material for MkDocs (squidfunk). Write documentation in Markdown, create professional static site in minutes. Searchable, customizable, 60+ languages. Built-in instant search, code annotations, social cards, 10,000+ icons and emojis. Fast and lightweight. Trusted by NASA, CERN, TC39, W3C, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, Font Awesome, Apache, freeCodeCamp. MIT.

Eleventy (11ty) v3.1.6 stable, v4.0.0-alpha.7 canary. 'a simpler static site generator.' Build benchmark: 4000 Markdown files in Eleventy 1.93s, Astro 22.90s, Gatsby 29.05s, Next.js 70.65s. 18.7M downloads, 82K+ dependent repos. Multiple template languages. Zero client-side JavaScript by default. No telemetry.

Astro Content Collections (added in astro@2.0.0). Two types: build-time and live. Loaders: glob() (for .md, .mdx, .markdoc, .json, .yaml, .toml directories) and file() (single files). Zod schema validation with TypeScript type-safety. astro:content runtime API: getCollection(), getEntry(), render(). Migration guides for Hugo, Jekyll, Gatsby, Next.js, Eleventy, etc.

MDN: CSS scroll-driven animations module. 'provides functionality that builds on top of the CSS animations module and Web Animations API. It allows you to animate property values along a scroll-based timeline rather than the default time-based document timeline.' Two types: scroll progress timeline and view progress timeline. Reference: animation-range, scroll-timeline, view-timeline, timeline-scope, scroll(), view(). Page last modified 2026-05-28.

Chrome for Developers: 'Animate elements on scroll with Scroll-driven animations' by Bramus (May 5, 2023). 'From Chrome version 115 there is a new set of APIs and concepts that you can use to enable declarative scroll-driven animations: Scroll Timelines and View Timelines.' Scroll Progress Timeline + View Progress Timeline. Runs off main thread. Browser support: Chrome 115+, Edge 115+, Firefox 'behind a flag', Safari 26.
CanIUse: css-animation-timeline. Chrome 4-114: Not supported. Chrome 115-147: Supported. Edge 12-114: Not supported. Edge 115-147: Supported. Independent confirmation of the Chrome 115+ baseline.

MDN: Intersection Observer API. 'Baseline Widely available. Available across browsers since March 2019.' 'Asynchronously observes changes in the intersection of a target element with an ancestor element or with a top-level document's viewport.' Options: root, rootMargin, scrollMargin, threshold, delay, trackVisibility. Page last modified 2026-01-12.

MDN: @starting-style CSS at-rule. Defines starting values for properties on an element that you want to transition from when first rendered (including from display: none). Now in Baseline (2024). Caveat: @starting-style rules must be placed AFTER post-entry state declarations (as of Chrome 130).

Chrome for Developers: 'Four new CSS features for smooth entry and exit animations' by Una Kravets and Joey Arhar. Chrome 116: display and content-visibility in keyframes. Chrome 117: transition-behavior: allow-discrete, @starting-style, overlay property. Updated 2024-09-13 for nested @starting-style rule ordering. As of Chrome 130, @starting-style rules must be placed after post-entry state declarations.

Chrome for Developers: 'Smooth transitions with the View Transition API'. 'Same-document view transitions are supported in Chrome from Chrome 111. Cross-document view transitions are supported in Chrome 126.' document.startViewTransition() pattern. Pseudo-elements: ::view-transition, ::view-transition-group, ::view-transition-image-pair, ::view-transition-old, ::view-transition-new. view-transition-name is the CSS property for snapshot targets.

MDN: Web Animations API. Framework underneath all of CSS animations, scroll-driven animations, and View Transitions. Element.animate() method, Animation interface, KeyframeEffect, AnimationTimeline, DocumentTimeline. JS-level control over animations that mirror CSS @keyframes + animation properties.

Gwern.net 'Design Of This Website' (modified 2023-04-20). Static website compiled via Hakyll from Pandoc Markdown. 4 design principles: aesthetically-pleasing minimalism, accessibility/progressive-enhancement, speed, 'semantic zoom' approach to hypertext. JavaScript not required for core reading experience. Inspirations: English Wikipedia, Project Xanadu, Tufte, Robert Bringhurst, Susan Kare, Donald Knuth, Markdeep, Andy Matuschak evergreen notes, Dieter Rams, Jakob Nielsen.

Tufte CSS by Dave Liepmann. Sidenotes, margin notes, careful typography. h1 title, h2 section, h3 low-level (cap at 3). Colors #fffff8 / #111111. ETBook serif with Palatino/Georgia fallback. 'The Feynman lectures use only 2 levels of hierarchical headings' (Tufte). newthought span for first few words of section in small caps. 'The web is not print. Webpages are not books. The goal of Tufte CSS is not to say websites should look like this interpretation of Tufte's books but rather here are some techniques Tufte developed that we've found useful in print.'

Chrome for Developers: 'CSS text-wrap: pretty'. 'text-wrap: pretty is for generally better line wrapping and text breaking, currently focused on orphans.' 'text-wrap: balance' is for headings and short blocks; 'text-wrap: pretty' is for paragraphs to avoid orphans. Available Chrome 117+, WebKit/Safari. Part of Baseline 2024.
WebKit blog: 'Better typography with text-wrap pretty' (2025). WebKit implementation confirming cross-browser support (Chrome 117+, Safari/WebKit, Firefox 121+).

HTTP Archive Web Almanac 2025: Page Weight. Median desktop home page 2.86MB (HTML 22KB, CSS 82KB, fonts 139KB, JS 697KB, images 1058KB). Median mobile home page 2.56MB. YoY growth 7.8% desktop. 90th percentile desktop 9.18MB. 'All key information should be present in the initial raw HTML, since AI crawlers do not render JavaScript.' Page weight implications: 100KB JS = significant CPU work; AI crawlers bias toward lighter-footprint web content.

Web Vitals (web.dev canonical, last updated 2024-10-31 by Philip Walton). Core Web Vitals (all Stable as of 2024): LCP < 2.5s (loading), INP <= 200ms (interactivity), CLS <= 0.1 (visual stability). 75th percentile is the measurement target. INP replaced FID in 2024. web-vitals JS library: `import {onCLS, onINP, onLCP} from 'web-vitals'`.

web-platform-dx features registry. scroll-driven-animations feature: 'baseline: false. support: chrome: 115.' Independent confirmation of the Chrome 115+ baseline from the official web-platform-dx project. Source for the 'baseline: false' classification (not yet cross-browser at 30+ months).

Anthropic: 'Introducing Claude 3.5 Sonnet' (Jun 21, 2024). 'We're also introducing Artifacts on Claude.ai, a new feature that expands how users can interact with Claude. When a user asks Claude to generate content like code snippets, text documents, or website designs, these Artifacts appear in a dedicated window alongside their conversation. This creates a dynamic workspace where they can see, edit, and build upon Claude's creations in real-time. This preview feature marks Claude's evolution from a conversational AI to a collaborative work environment.' Made generally available Aug 28, 2024.

Perplexity blog: 'Introducing Perplexity Pages' (May 30, 2024). 'Meet Perplexity Pages, your new tool for easily transforming research into visually stunning, comprehensive content.' Quick content generation, highly customizable, media insertion, source validation and citation, SEO-optimized. Initial Pro users, then rolled out to all users. The 'AI Wikipedia' framing.

Tencent News (secondary coverage of Anthropic engineer Thariq's viral 'MD is dead, long live HTML' thread, May 12, 2026). Thariq argues: (1) Information density: HTML supports tables, CSS styles, SVG, JS interaction, canvas; MD only headings, bold, lists, code blocks. (2) Readability: 'MD, sex appeal is exhausted.' (3) HTML5 brings back control. (4) AI agents output HTML more naturally. (5) HTML closes the gap between AI and human authoring. Karpathy endorsed. Secondary source — original Thariq thread on X not directly fetched. Watching for further corroboration.

Mavis skill: content-deck-generator v1.0.0. Reads ~/.mavis/fleet/*-draft.md, parses X (tweet thread) and LinkedIn (longform) buckets, renders standalone dark-theme HTML deck with numbered tweet cards and LinkedIn block, Copy buttons, 60s auto-refresh via + inline countdown. Output: ~/.mavis/fleet/outbound/content-deck.html. Theme: Tony Stark Dark Arc Reactor (--bg #0d0d0d, --card-bg #1a1a2e, --accent #00aaff, --accent2 #ff3d00). Card:
N
...
. LinkedIn 1.7 line-height. Mobile responsive (1-col below 700px).

Mavis skill: html-presentation-generator. Multi-page HTML presentations exportable to PDF/PPTX. 5 slide types: cover, TOC, section divider, content pages, summary/closing. Fixed 960x540 dimensions per slide. Font: Times New Roman. All CSS inline. 'No animations: No CSS animations, transitions, hover effects, or SVG animations.' SVG for decorative shapes. Page number badge mandatory on all slides except cover. Image generation mandatory for cover and content pages.

Mavis skill: landing-page-builder. Awwwards-level landing pages with cinematic hero, deployed online. Spec-first workflow: gather requirements, generate spec.md (expanded prompt + tech strategy + design system), produce HTML/CSS/JS, deploy. Hero Section is Video-First. Strict color constraint. Strict palette + typography discipline. Stack: HTML5 + modern CSS + minimal JS.

MDN: View Transition API. Same-document view transitions supported Chrome 111+. Cross-document supported Chrome 126+. Pattern: document.startViewTransition(() => { DOM mutation }). Pseudo-elements: ::view-transition, ::view-transition-group, ::view-transition-image-pair, ::view-transition-old, ::view-transition-new. view-transition-name is the CSS property for snapshot targets. Firefox supports same-document only as of mid-2024.

MDN: @starting-style CSS at-rule. Defines starting values for properties on an element you want to transition from when first rendered (including from display: none). Now in Baseline (2024). Caveat: @starting-style rules must be placed AFTER post-entry state declarations (as of Chrome 130). For v1: overkill; we can use plain @keyframes + animation-delay + IntersectionObserver.

MDN: Web Animations API. The framework underneath all CSS animations, scroll-driven animations, and View Transitions. Element.animate() method, Animation interface, KeyframeEffect, AnimationTimeline, DocumentTimeline.

Mavis skill: visual-summary. 'Proactively create a visual summary HTML page when plain text cannot effectively convey the information. Use this skill when the content involves diagrams (flowcharts, architecture, sequence diagrams), data comparisons (tables, charts), timelines, interactive demos, visual layouts, or any scenario where a simple webpage would communicate more clearly than markdown text.' Triggers: 'visual summary', 'visualize', 'animate', 'demonstrate', 'interactive demo'. NOT currently installed at the standard path — directory exists but is empty. The trigger logic is the right precedent; our dossier rendering is the broader generalization.

web.dev: 'Now in Baseline: animating entry effects' (2024). Confirms @starting-style and transition-behavior: allow-discrete are now Baseline. 'The @starting-style rule defines the initial styles on an element before it's rendered on the page. This is required for elements that animate in.'

W3C/CSSWG Scroll-driven Animations spec. Drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1. Defines scroll-timeline and view-timeline properties. Reference for the canonical implementation. Independent cross-references: scroll-timeline-axis, scroll-timeline-name, view-timeline-inset, animation-range-start, animation-range-end.

CommonMark. 'A rationalized version of Markdown syntax with a spec.' Created by John MacFarlane (jgm), 2014. Underlying spec for GFM and most modern parsers. 600+ conformance tests. ATX headings, Setext headings, indented code blocks (4 spaces), fenced code blocks (3+ backticks or tildes), blockquotes, ordered/unordered lists, link reference definitions, backslash escapes, hard/soft line breaks.

Federal Register notice 91 FR 20465-20467 (April 16, 2026), Doc. 2026-07361, Docket No. FDA-2025-N-6895. 'Pharmacy Compounding Advisory Committee; Notice of Meeting; Establishment of a Public Docket; Request for Comments-Bulk Drug Substances Nominated for Inclusion on the Section 503A Bulk Drug Substances List.' Signed by Grace R. Graham, Deputy Commissioner for Policy, Legislation, and International Affairs. Filed 4-15-26; 8:45 am. Sets PCAC meeting July 23-24, 2026, 8:00 a.m. - 4:30 p.m. ET (July 23) and 8:00 a.m. - 3:50 p.m. ET (July 24) at FDA White Oak Campus, Bldg. 31, Rm. 1503, Silver Spring, MD. Docket close July 22, 2026; comments to Committee by July 9, 2026; oral presentation requests by June 30, 2026. Agenda: July 23 = BPC-157, KPV, TB-500, MOTs-C; July 24 = Emideltide (DSIP), Semax, Epitalon. All being considered for inclusion on the 503A Bulks List. Each substance has an evaluated use (UC, wound healing, opioid withdrawal, insomnia, etc.). Contact: Takyiah Stevenson, CDER, 240-402-2507, PCAC@fda.hhs.gov.

FDA Advisory Committee Calendar event page for July 23-24, 2026 PCAC meeting. Center: CDER. Location: FDA White Oak Campus, Bldg. 31, Rm. 1503, 10903 New Hampshire Ave., Silver Spring, MD 20903. Content current as of 04/15/2026. Confirms 7-peptide agenda across 2 days (4 on July 23, 3 on July 24) being considered for inclusion on the 503A Bulks List. Use-evaluated table matches Federal Register text. Background material to be posted no later than 2 business days before meeting. Public Participation section: written comments to Docket FDA-2025-N-6895 by July 22, 2026; to Committee by July 9, 2026. NOTE: One instruction paragraph in Public Participation Information references 'Docket No. FDA-2026-N-2979' — apparent typo (FR notice and Regulations.gov both confirm FDA-2025-N-6895). Top-of-page time '8:00 a.m. - 3:50 p.m. ET' is day-unspecific and conflicts slightly with FR's per-day 4:30/3:50 p.m. split — FR is authoritative.

FDA 'Bulk Drug Substances Used in Compounding Under Section 503A of the FD&C Act' hub page. Content current as of 05/14/2026. Defines 3-category interim policy framework: Category 1 (eligible, no significant safety risks, FDA does not intend to take action), Category 2 (significant safety risks, NOT in scope of enforcement policy, FDA would consider action), Category 3 (insufficient supporting information, not eligible for category 1 policy). 503A conditions: bulk drug substance must (a) comply with USP/NF monograph, (b) be component of FDA-approved drug, or (c) appear on 503A bulks list. Plus: valid certificate of analysis, manufacturer registered under section 510, compounding pursuant to valid prescription for individually identified patient. Guidance states FDA does not intend to place substances nominated on or after January 7, 2025 into these categories (interim policy revision).

Regulations.gov docket FDA-2025-N-6895 (the comment-collection docket for the April 16, 2026 Federal Register notice). Comment Due Date 07/22/2026. 65 comments received. Docket title: 'Pharmacy Compounding Advisory Committee; Notice of Meeting; Establishment of a Public Docket; Request for Comments--Bulk Drug Substances Nominated for Inclusion on the Section 503A Bulk Drug Substances List.' Document ID: FDA-2025-N-6895-0001.

Frier Levitt (FDA regulatory law firm) article, dated April 16, 2026. Authors: Guilherme Ferrari Faviero (Senior Associate) and Edgar J. Asebey (Partner). 'FDA to Remove 12 Popular Peptides from the Category 2 "Do Not Compound" List.' Key facts: FDA on April 15 published the meeting notice + republished 503A Bulks List with 12-peptide Category 2 removal within 7 calendar days; announcement does NOT place peptides on Category 1 'May Compound' list. Kennedy's Feb 27, 2026 Rogan statement cited as political context (~14 peptides expected). Full 12-peptide enumeration: BPC-157, Cathelicidin LL-37, Dihexa Acetate, Emideltide (DSIP), Epitalon, GHK-Cu (injectable), KPV, Mechano Growth Factor Pegylated (PEG-MGF), Melanotan II, MOTs-C, Semax (heptapeptide), TB-500. GHK-Cu non-injectable also removed from Category 1. 5 substances NOT on July agenda (Cathelicidin LL-37, Dihexa Acetate, GHK-Cu injectable, PEG-MGF, Melanotan II) slated for second PCAC consultation before end of February 2027. PCAC currently has only 4 members (lacks quorum); several prior members removed and not replaced. 2024 PCAC voted against inclusion of all previously reviewed peptides. Removal from Category 2 does NOT place substances on 503A bulks list or in Category 1; 'regulatory gray area' until PCAC + FDA act.

JD Supra / Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP article, dated April 17, 2026. Authors: Shari Esfahani, Thora Johnson, Amy Joseph, Georgia Ravitz, Jeremy Sherer. 'FDA Announces Removal of 12 Peptides from Category 2 and Schedules PCAC Meetings to Consider Adding Peptides to 503A Bulk Drug Substances List.' Corroborates Frier Levitt on 12-peptide removal, July 23-24 PCAC meeting, and second meeting before end of February 2027. Adds: Feb 27, 2026 Kennedy Rogan-appearance quote ('big fan' of peptides, said Biden 'illegally' reclassified 19 peptides into Category 2 in September 2023, expected announcement 'within a couple of weeks'). 2024 PCAC precedent: voted against inclusion of all previously reviewed peptides. PCAC currently has significant vacancies Secretary Kennedy could fill. 'If the PCAC recommends inclusion and the FDA includes the peptides on the 503A bulks list, pharmacies holding state licenses and meeting all other 503A conditions would be authorized to prepare patient-specific formulations of the included peptides — a substantial commercial opportunity. To comply with section 503A, pharmacies will need to source pharmaceutical-grade API from FDA-registered manufacturers, accompanied by valid certificates of analysis.' Removal from Category 2 effective 'approximately by April 22, 2026.' PCAC recommendations advisory; FDA retains accept/modify/reject authority. Compounds should not begin compounding with any of the 12 solely on basis of Category 2 removal.

MiniMax M3 launch blog (June 1, 2026). MSA architecture, 1M context, native multimodality (image+video), desktop computer use, MoE 196B total / 11B active, 400 TPS. SWE-Bench Pro 59% (beats GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, approaches Opus 4.7), SVG-Bench beats Opus 4.7, OmniDocBench beats Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claw-Eval highest score. Compatible with Claude Code and MCP protocol, supports cloud and local deployment.
MiniMax M3 product page. Frontier-level performance on coding and agentic tasks, 1M context via MSA, native multimodal.
Medium analysis confirming M3 is first open-weights model to combine frontier coding + 1M context + native multimodality + computer use.
China Tech coverage: M3 single-token compute at 1M context is 1/20th of prior generation. New data-read and compute-path redesign yields 4x+ performance vs mainstream open-source solutions.

Detailed M3 spec breakdown: max 1M tokens (at least 512K stable), MoE 196B/11B active, MSA sparse attention, native multimodal (image/video), tools/agent/multimodal API, available via API and platform, weights planned open-source.
Tencent News coverage confirming M3 launch details: 1M context, native multimodal, desktop operation, 400 TPS, open-weights, MCP/Claude Code compatible, cloud + local deployment.

Claude Code computer use docs (Mac). Built-in MCP server integration, activated via /mcp command, no Docker/VM required. Requires Claude Code v2.1.85+, macOS, Pro/Max subscription. Tool selection priority: MCP > shell > Chrome extension > computer use last-resort.
Anthropic's official docs on packaging MCP servers as Claude Desktop Extensions (.dxt files) for one-click install. Walk-through uses filesystem server as canonical example.
The New Stack guide to configuring Claude Desktop with filesystem MCP server. Demonstrates local file read/write/rename/move via natural language.
Step-by-step guide to MCP filesystem access in Claude Desktop. Documents the read/write permission model and path restrictions.

NetEase 163 article on Obsidian+Claude connection: Obsidian is a folder of plain Markdown files. Claude Desktop's filesystem connector needs only 3 clicks to wire. Many Obsidian-specific MCP servers exist for deeper graph/plugin integration, but filesystem is the simple path.

Juejin article on MinerU MCP Server architecture. MCP = protocol-level tool integration (vs SDK-level), open-sourced by Anthropic Nov 2024, donated to Linux Foundation 2025-12, now >97M monthly downloads, 4000+ registered servers.

Empirical comparison: CLI agents vs MCP agents. CLI 10-32x cheaper in tokens, near-100% reliability; MCP 72% reliability due to schema injection overhead. Anthropic internal research: shell scripts vs MCP tools = 98.7% token reduction. Three servers = 150K token overhead before task starts.

Jianshu 'Production AI Agent Construction Guide'. Anthropic's David Soria Parra: MCP downloads hit 110M/month, faster growth than React. Three-layer connection stack: Skills (domain knowledge) + CLI/Computer Use (local execution, ~200 tokens/response) + MCP (connectivity with OAuth/governance/audit).
OSWorld-MCP GitHub repo: first comprehensive benchmark for assessing computer-use agents' tool invocation, GUI operation, and decision-making. Joint measurement of MCP tool use + GUI primitives.

OSWorld-MCP arXiv paper (Jia et al. Oct 2025). Tool-invocation dimension added to OSWorld: agents using MCP tools score higher than pure-GUI agents. Real workflows mix both; harness matters as much as the model.
Stanford HAI 2026 AI Index Report: OSWorld AI agent success rate rose from 12% (2025) to 66.3% (2026-03). Human baseline 72-74%. 1-year jump: 10x8-failure to 10x3-failure on real desktop tasks.

Agent-Native Memory Through LLM-Curated Hierarchical Context (arXiv 2604.01599). Introduces the Context Tree: a hierarchical file-based knowledge graph with Adaptive Knowledge Lifecycle (AKL) — importance scoring, maturity levels, file-tree paging.

AdaGReS (Adaptive Greedy Context Selection, arXiv 2512.25052, Dec 2025). Redundancy-aware context selection for token-budgeted RAG. Greedy selection with marginal gains from query-chunk relevance + intra-set redundancy penalties.

TeaRAG (arXiv 2511.05385, Nov 2025). Token-Efficient Agentic RAG framework. Addresses the trade-off that agentic RAG improves via RL but incurs substantial token overhead from search/reasoning. Token-efficiency is the explicit optimization target.

Meta Superintelligence Lab first paper on context-intelligent compression. REFRAG framework: 30.85x TTFT speedup, 16x context length extension. Selectively preserves key chunks' original tokens; compresses low-relevance chunks via embeddings.
Moonshot context-caching public beta. 90% cost reduction, 83% first-token latency reduction for repeated large-doc QA. Cache stable for 10 minutes; suitable for fixed-context multi-turn workflows.
LlamaIndex blog on Long Context RAG architectures. New LLM designs (Gemini 1.5 Pro etc.) need intelligent routing + efficient KV caching, not just larger windows.
Letta/MemGPT Memory Blocks paper. Structures context into discrete functional units (memory blocks) that the LLM agent can read/write/pin. Paging abstraction over the context window.

PagedEviction (arXiv 2509.04377, 2025). Block-wise eviction algorithm for PagedAttention. Drops lowest-importance blocks by ||V||/||K|| ratio. 10-12% lower latency, 15-20% better accuracy than StreamingLLM.
LangChain blog: harness engineering moved their coding agent from outside top 30 to rank 5 on Terminal Bench 2.0 (same model). Self-verification + tracing + context optimization were the levers.

Phil Schmid: 'The importance of Agent Harness in 2026'. Harness will become the primary tool for solving model drift — labs use the harness to detect exactly when a model stops following expected behavior patterns.

Juejin 2026 Harness Engineering retrospective. OpenAI disclosed Codex built 1M+ lines production app with zero human-written code; harness = constraints + feedback loops + guardrails. Anthropic uses 3-agent planning+generation+evaluation harness. Martin Fowler: Agent = Model + Harness.

Fudan University Agentic Harness Engineering (AHE). On Terminal-Bench 2.0 with GPT-5.4: AHE 77.0 vs baseline 69.7 (+7.3 points). Self-evolution via observability-driven harness optimization. Auto-adapts to new model versions (GPT-5.5 → ranked #3 globally).

2026 Industry agent intent-routing pattern: layered funnel (regex → vector → LLM). Cursor/Claude Code/Feishu/Linux: regex intercepts 80% of fixed commands at zero latency. Three-tier funnel is now the standard.
LLM-as-router pattern: slash commands and safe compound commands stay deterministic, natural language goes through classifier. Three-tier routing reference implementation for production agents.
Eval harness as the gate between agent prototype and production. Golden-test suite + CI wiring + drift detection = the eval-vault pattern.
Letta Agent Memory. Techniques: message eviction + summarization when context exceeds flush threshold. Recursive summarization preserves continuity without losing fidelity.

AnchorAttention implementation (Zhang et al. arXiv 2505.23520). Difference-aware sparse attention with stripe granularity. Long-context training improvement: plug-and-play attention mechanism that handles BFloat16 RoPE breakdown.

MiniMax M3 launch blog (June 1, 2026). MSA architecture, 1M context, native multimodality (image+video), desktop computer use, MoE 196B total / 11B active, 400 TPS. SWE-Bench Pro 59% (beats GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, approaches Opus 4.7), SVG-Bench beats Opus 4.7, OmniDocBench beats Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claw-Eval highest score. Compatible with Claude Code and MCP protocol, supports cloud and local deployment.
MiniMax M3 product page. Frontier-level performance on coding and agentic tasks, 1M context via MSA, native multimodal.
Medium analysis confirming M3 is first open-weights model to combine frontier coding + 1M context + native multimodality + computer use.
China Tech coverage: M3 single-token compute at 1M context is 1/20th of prior generation. New data-read and compute-path redesign yields 4x+ performance vs mainstream open-source solutions.

Detailed M3 spec breakdown: max 1M tokens (at least 512K stable), MoE 196B/11B active, MSA sparse attention, native multimodal (image/video), tools/agent/multimodal API, available via API and platform, weights planned open-source.
Tencent News coverage confirming M3 launch details: 1M context, native multimodal, desktop operation, 400 TPS, open-weights, MCP/Claude Code compatible, cloud + local deployment.

Claude Code computer use docs (Mac). Built-in MCP server integration, activated via /mcp command, no Docker/VM required. Requires Claude Code v2.1.85+, macOS, Pro/Max subscription. Tool selection priority: MCP > shell > Chrome extension > computer use last-resort.
Anthropic's official docs on packaging MCP servers as Claude Desktop Extensions (.dxt files) for one-click install. Walk-through uses filesystem server as canonical example.
The New Stack guide to configuring Claude Desktop with filesystem MCP server. Demonstrates local file read/write/rename/move via natural language.
Step-by-step guide to MCP filesystem access in Claude Desktop. Documents the read/write permission model and path restrictions.

NetEase 163 article on Obsidian+Claude connection: Obsidian is a folder of plain Markdown files. Claude Desktop's filesystem connector needs only 3 clicks to wire. Many Obsidian-specific MCP servers exist for deeper graph/plugin integration, but filesystem is the simple path.

Juejin article on MinerU MCP Server architecture. MCP = protocol-level tool integration (vs SDK-level), open-sourced by Anthropic Nov 2024, donated to Linux Foundation 2025-12, now >97M monthly downloads, 4000+ registered servers.

Empirical comparison: CLI agents vs MCP agents. CLI 10-32x cheaper in tokens, near-100% reliability; MCP 72% reliability due to schema injection overhead. Anthropic internal research: shell scripts vs MCP tools = 98.7% token reduction. Three servers = 150K token overhead before task starts.

Jianshu 'Production AI Agent Construction Guide'. Anthropic's David Soria Parra: MCP downloads hit 110M/month, faster growth than React. Three-layer connection stack: Skills (domain knowledge) + CLI/Computer Use (local execution, ~200 tokens/response) + MCP (connectivity with OAuth/governance/audit).
OSWorld-MCP GitHub repo: first comprehensive benchmark for assessing computer-use agents' tool invocation, GUI operation, and decision-making. Joint measurement of MCP tool use + GUI primitives.

OSWorld-MCP arXiv paper (Jia et al. Oct 2025). Tool-invocation dimension added to OSWorld: agents using MCP tools score higher than pure-GUI agents. Real workflows mix both; harness matters as much as the model.
Stanford HAI 2026 AI Index Report: OSWorld AI agent success rate rose from 12% (2025) to 66.3% (2026-03). Human baseline 72-74%. 1-year jump: 10x8-failure to 10x3-failure on real desktop tasks.

Agent-Native Memory Through LLM-Curated Hierarchical Context (arXiv 2604.01599). Introduces the Context Tree: a hierarchical file-based knowledge graph with Adaptive Knowledge Lifecycle (AKL) — importance scoring, maturity levels, file-tree paging.

AdaGReS (Adaptive Greedy Context Selection, arXiv 2512.25052, Dec 2025). Redundancy-aware context selection for token-budgeted RAG. Greedy selection with marginal gains from query-chunk relevance + intra-set redundancy penalties.

TeaRAG (arXiv 2511.05385, Nov 2025). Token-Efficient Agentic RAG framework. Addresses the trade-off that agentic RAG improves via RL but incurs substantial token overhead from search/reasoning. Token-efficiency is the explicit optimization target.

Meta Superintelligence Lab first paper on context-intelligent compression. REFRAG framework: 30.85x TTFT speedup, 16x context length extension. Selectively preserves key chunks' original tokens; compresses low-relevance chunks via embeddings.
Moonshot context-caching public beta. 90% cost reduction, 83% first-token latency reduction for repeated large-doc QA. Cache stable for 10 minutes; suitable for fixed-context multi-turn workflows.
LlamaIndex blog on Long Context RAG architectures. New LLM designs (Gemini 1.5 Pro etc.) need intelligent routing + efficient KV caching, not just larger windows.
Letta/MemGPT Memory Blocks paper. Structures context into discrete functional units (memory blocks) that the LLM agent can read/write/pin. Paging abstraction over the context window.

PagedEviction (arXiv 2509.04377, 2025). Block-wise eviction algorithm for PagedAttention. Drops lowest-importance blocks by ||V||/||K|| ratio. 10-12% lower latency, 15-20% better accuracy than StreamingLLM.
LangChain blog: harness engineering moved their coding agent from outside top 30 to rank 5 on Terminal Bench 2.0 (same model). Self-verification + tracing + context optimization were the levers.
